title: Geography of Liberia
content:
Liberia is a sub-Saharan nation in West Africa located at 6 °N, 9 °W. It borders the north Atlantic Ocean to the southwest (of coastline) and three other African nations on the other three sides, Sierra Leone to the northwest, Guinea to the northeast and Ivory Coast (Côte d'Ivoire) to the east. In total, Liberia comprises of which is land and is water. Physical geography.

Liberia extends from between 4.21°N and 8.34°N to 7.27°W and 11.31°W. It is roughly rectangular measuring about in length from northwest to southeast, with a width of about. The coastline is about, including river mouths ad inlets up to one kilometre wide. Drainage of the whole country is direct to the sea, with a series of short rivers flowing directly into the sea.
